title: v1.18.0 Release Planning
body:
**Release Tracking Board:** [https://github.com/orgs/dapr/projects/98/views/1]() **Release lead:** @nelson-parente
**Release lead shadow(s):** TBD
**Performance test lead:** @javier-aliaga
**Longhaul test lead:** @cicoyle
**Build manager:** @mikeee Refer to the [release lifecycle documentation]() for more details. ## Milestone Dates * **Feature definition starts:** 24/03/2026 * **Feature definition ends:** April 14th * **Feature freeze:** April 21st * **Code freeze (Runtime only; Endgame starts \~4 weeks stabilization):** April 28th * **RC release day:** April 28th * **Release day:** May 26th * **Announcement day:** May 26th ## Priorities/Themes for This Release * \[P0\] * Workflow Context Propagation * Workflow external event timer * History Signing * MCP Server Support * Workflow Access Policy * \[P1\] * Migrate DurableTask Repos into SDK Repos * Vector and Search stores * Add workflow global and per-name concurrency limits ## Main Features Overview ### \[P0\] Workflow Context Propagation (dapr/dapr#9721) **Owner:** @cicoyle
**Proposal:** [dapr/proposals#102]() — Workflow History propagation & signing Enable workflow context propagation so that trace context and other metadata flows correctly between workflow orchestrations, activities, and child workflows. * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9810]() * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-go/pull/85]() * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-protobuf/pull/38]() * Follow-up: wire up signing and propagation * SDKs: * [https://github.com/dapr/dotnet-sdk/issues/1801]() * *Missing*: SDKs Issues + Docs + quickstarts ### \[P0\] Workflow external event timer (dapr/dapr#9722) **Owner:** @acroca
**Proposal:** [dapr/proposals#104]() — Workflow external event timer tracking Add external event timer support to Dapr Workflows, allowing workflows to pause and wait for external human input or approval before continuing execution. Proposal approved, protos merged, runtime and durabletask-go PRs open. * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-protobuf/pull/34]() * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-protobuf/pull/37]() * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-go/pull/80]() * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-go/pull/81]() * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-go/pull/82]() * [https://github.com/dapr/cli/pull/1627]() * SDKs: * [https://github.com/dapr/python-sdk/pull/976]() * [https://github.com/dapr/java-sdk/pull/1733]() * [https://github.com/dapr/dotnet-sdk/pull/1790]() * Docs: https://github.com/dapr/docs/pull/5197 ### \[P0\] History Signing (dapr/dapr#9723) **Owner:** @JoshVanL
**Proposal:** [dapr/proposals#102]() — Workflow History propagation & signing Implement workflow history signing to ensure the integrity and authenticity of workflow execution history, preventing tampering with completed workflow steps. Protos merged, implementation underway. * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-protobuf/pull/35]() * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-go/pull/77]() * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9778]() * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9825]() * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-protobuf/pull/41]() * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-go/pull/87]() * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9831]() * **No SDK work required** * *Missing*: Docs(?) + quickstarts ### \[P0\] MCP Server Support (dapr/dapr#9724) **Owner:** @sicoyle Add MCP (Model Context Protocol) server support to Dapr, enabling AI tools and agents to interact with Dapr building blocks (state, pub/sub, service invocation, workflows, etc.) through a unified MCP interface. Continues work from dapr/dapr#8783. PR open, under review. * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-protobuf/pull/39]() * [https://github.com/dapr/durabletask-go/pull/83]() * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9731]() * [https://github.com/dapr/docs/pull/5129]() * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9685]() * **Nice to have**: SDK wrappers (create issues) + quickstarts ### \[P0\] Workflow Access Policy (dapr/dapr#9725) **Owners:** @JoshVanL, @javier-aliaga
**Proposal:** [dapr/proposals#101]() — WorkflowAccessPolicy Introduce access policies for Dapr Workflows, allowing operators to define fine-grained authorization rules controlling which applications can start, query, or manage specific workflows. Early iteration. * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9790]() * [https://github.com/dapr/docs/pull/5105]() * ***No SDK work required*** ### \[P1\] Migrate DurableTask Repos into SDK Repos (dapr/dapr#9726) **Owner:** @sicoyle Migrate the DurableTask client libraries into their corresponding Dapr SDK repos (Python, JS, and others) to reduce the extra hop when iterating on fixes. Python and JS iterating on feedback, Rust learnings shared with maintainers. ### \[P1\] Vector and Search stores **Owner:** @mikeee
**Proposal:** [https://github.com/dapr/proposals/pull/107]() Initial implementation of the search & vector stores with no deep integration with other building blocks(/ai) ### \[P1\] Add workflow global and per-name concurrency limits ([https://github.com/dapr/dapr/issues/9796]()) **Owner:** @JoshVanL Add scheduler-enforced concurrency limits for workflows and activities that apply across all replicas. * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9775]() * [https://github.com/dapr/docs/pull/5109]() * **No SDK work required** ## Additional Issues Additional issues targeted for the 1.18 release. - [ ] Implement actor APIs in gRPC ([#927]()) — add gRPC equivalents for actor HTTP APIs, SDK support needed (.NET) * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9812]() * *Missing*: SDK issues + Docs + quickstarts - [ ] Move subscription hot reload to GA (dapr/dapr#9577) — move from preview to stable * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9811]() * *Missing*: Docs - [ ] Promote Dapr Jobs API to Stable (dapr/dapr#9257) — move Jobs API from alpha to stable (in progress, @cicoyle) * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9706]() * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9711]() * SDKs: * [https://github.com/dapr/dotnet-sdk/pull/1804]() * *Missing*: SDK issues + Docs + quickstarts - - [ ] Add first-class support to trigger new, orphaned child workflows from a parent (dapr/dapr#9261) * @acroca to scope and clarify what will be done. From that we make a decision. ## Repo Milestones * [https://github.com/dapr/dapr/milestone/27]() * [https://github.com/dapr/components-contrib/milestone/20]() * [https://github.com/dapr/docs/milestone/21]() * [https://github.com/dapr/cli/milestone/19]() * [https://github.com/dapr/dotnet-sdk/milestone/24]() * [https://github.com/dapr/java-sdk/milestone/19]() * [https://github.com/dapr/python-sdk/milestone/21]() * [https://github.com/dapr/js-sdk/milestone/22]() ## Grafana Dashboard - Longhauls TBD Read about the [Dapr release lifecycle]()

comments.body:
Breaking change https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9739
Removing support for workflow instance ID reuse policy

**_(ai generated)_**
**1.18 Kickoff Recap — March 31**

Features in progress:                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
  - Context propagation (Cassie) — proto rename prerequisite first
  - Human in the Loop (Albert) — protos merged, runtime PRs open  
  - History signing (Josh) — implementation underway            
  - MCP Server (Sam) — PR open, new CRD resource                                                                                                                                                                                           
  - Access policy (Josh + Javi) — early exploration
  - DurableTask SDK migration (Sam) — Python done, JS/Rust in progress

Key decisions: proto rename before context propagation, keep Microsoft copyright on DurableTask code, feature definition closes next meeting with time estimates from all owners.

**_(ai generated)_**
Last release meeting (Apr 7):
  - 1.18 confirmed as feature-driven release — commit to date when features are done
  - Estimates: history signing Apr 11, access policy Apr 17, HitL ~Apr 14, context propagation Apr 24 (longest), MCP Server CRD no estimate (Sam absent)
  - Whit is build manager
  - CVE (ACL bypass) flagged — no release date set
  - Perf + long haul tests broken (Azure subscription) — no owner
  - Context propagation sets the lower bound for the release date
